name: Xon

hero.title: Xon — Developer-Friendly Configuration
hero.summary: Xon is a modern configuration language that extends JSON with practical features like comments, trailing commas, and native expression evaluation. Built for real-world use cases with a production-ready C API and Node.js bindings.
features: Trailing commas in objects and lists
Native C parser with zero dependencies
privacy: Xon is a local development tool and library.
It does not collect, transmit, or store personal telemetry.
dataUsage: Data parsed by Xon remains entirely on your local machine or server.
We do not track schema usage or configuration contents.
userControlSecurity: You have full control over your configuration deployment and execution.
Xon's C API allows you to restrict runtime evaluation limits for secure parsing.
Industry-standard compilation and memory-safety practices are applied.
hero.ownershipOperator: Xon is developed and operated by Xerxisfy LLC. This project is open-source under the MIT license.

description: Xon is a modern configuration language that extends JSON with comments, hex numbers, trailing commas, and more. Built in C with a hand-written lexer and LALR(1) parser, Xon is designed for real-world use cases and offers a branded, production-ready C API and Node.js bindings.

Language Features: Trailing commas in objects and lists
Developer Experience: Type-safe API for objects, lists, numbers, strings, booleans, null
Native Node.js bindings with TypeScript support
CLI tool for parsing and validating .xon files
Performance & Usability: Clear error messages with line numbers
Visitor pattern for dynamic data traversal

reviews.comment: Xon makes configuration files readable, maintainable, and powerful for both C and JavaScript projects.
Finally, a config language with comments and trailing commas that works natively in C and Node.js!

Description: Use in C: #include <xon_api.h> and call xonify()
Type checking: xon_get_type, xon_is_null, xon_is_bool, etc.
Xon supports comments, hex numbers, trailing commas, and unquoted keys.
Reach out for support, feedback, or contributions.

1.0.0: Initial release: JSON-compatible syntax, comments, hex numbers, trailing commas, unquoted keys.
Production-ready C library and CLI tool.

support.troubleshooting: Open an issue on GitHub for bugs or questions.
